description: This file controls the functionalities for armor type items. This file is loaded together with other similar files in the following order: weapons, armor, misc. These combined files form the items structure. Technically these files share the same fields, but some fields are exclusive for specific item types, so they are not displayed in the data files that do not need them. These are the fields exclusive to this file. Any column field name starting with "*" is considered a comment field and is not used by the game.
source: Colonnes du .txt (BKVince 3.2) ; types/descriptions du guide TXT eezstreet-d2rdoc.

columns.name: name
columns.description: Reference field to define the item.

columns.name: version
columns.description: Defines which game version to create this item (0 = Classic mode | 100 = Expansion mode).

columns.name: compactsave
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then only the item's base stats will be stored in the character save, but not any modifiers or additional stats. If equals 0, then all of the items stats will be saved.

columns.name: rarity
columns.description: Determines the chance that the item will randomly spawn (1/#). The higher the value then the rarer the item will be. This field depends on the .spawnable field being enabled, the .quest field being disabled, and the item level being less than or equal to the area level. This value is also affected by the relative Act number that the item is dropping in, where the higher the Act number, then the more common the item will drop.

columns.name: spawnable
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then this item can be randomly spawned. If equals 0, then this item will never randomly spawn. Community note: If this field is enabled, the item can still be created using the Horadric Cube.

columns.name: DropConditionCalc
columns.description: If the evaluated result of this condition is 0, then prevent the item from being randomly dropped in treasure classes. Treasure Class scope BBE. See BBE/Calc Fields

columns.name: minac
columns.description: The minimum amount of Defense that an armor item type can have.

columns.name: maxac
columns.description: The maximum amount of Defense that an armor item type can have.

columns.name: speed
columns.description: If the item type is an armor, then this will affect the Walk/Run Speed reduction when wearing the armor. If the item type is a weapon, then this will affect the Attack Speed reduction when wearing the weapon.

columns.name: reqstr
columns.description: Defines the amount of the Strength attribute needed to use the item.

columns.name: reqdex
columns.description: Defines the amount of the Dexterity attribute needed to use the item.

columns.name: block
columns.description: Controls the block percent chance that the item provides (out of 100, but caps at 75).

columns.name: durability
columns.description: Defines the base durability amount that the item will spawn with.

columns.name: nodurability
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then the item will not have durability. If equals 0, then the item will have durability.

columns.name: level
columns.description: Controls the base item level. This is used for determining when the item is allowed to drop, such as making sure that the item level is not greater than the monster's level or the area level.

columns.name: ShowLevel
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then display the item level next to the item name. If equals 0, then ignore this.

columns.name: levelreq
columns.description: Controls the player level requirement for being able to use the item.

columns.name: cost
columns.description: Defines the base gold cost of the item when being sold by an NPC. This can be affected by item modifiers and the rarity of the item. Community note: The rarity of the item has no bearing on the cost. This base cost is modified by the 'Reduced Vendor Costs' property and the costs of each stat as defined in itemstatcost.

columns.name: gamble cost
columns.description: Defines the gambling gold cost of the item on the Gambling UI.

columns.name: code
columns.description: Defines a unique 3 letter/number code for the item. This is used as an identifier to reference the item.

columns.name: namestr
columns.description: String Key that is used for the base item name.

columns.name: magic lvl
columns.description: Defines the magic level of the item, which can affect how magical item modifiers that can appear on the item (See automagic).

columns.name: auto prefix
columns.description: Automatically picks an item affix name from a designated automagic.group value, instead of using random prefixes. This is only used when the item is Magical quality. Community note: This description is inaccurate - it applies to normal (white/grey) items as well, and it doesn't replace random prefixes - it adds to them. This column needs to be set in order for automagic to work properly.

columns.name: alternategfx
columns.description: Uses a unique 3 letter/number code similar to the defined .code fields to determine what in-game graphics to display on the player character when the item is equipped.

columns.name: normcode
columns.description: Links to a .code field to determine the normal version of the item Community note: This column doesn't exist by default in misc.txt. It can be safely added. An item must have an entry in this column if it can be gambled, otherwise the game will crash. (Rings and amulets are a hardcoded exception.)

columns.name: ubercode
columns.description: Links to a .code field to determine the Exceptional version of the item Community note: This column doesn't exist by default in misc.txt. It can be safely added. An item must have an entry in this column if it can be gambled, otherwise the game will crash. (Rings and amulets are a hardcoded exception.)

columns.name: ultracode
columns.description: Links to a .code field to determine the Elite version of the item Community note: This column doesn't exist by default in misc.txt. It can be safely added. An item must have an entry in this column if it can be gambled, otherwise the game will crash. (Rings and amulets are a hardcoded exception.)

columns.name: component
columns.description: Determines the layer of player animation when the item is equipped. This uses a code referenced from the Composit file.

columns.name: invwidth
columns.description: Defines the width of grid cells that the item occupies in the player inventory.

columns.name: invheight
columns.description: Defines the height of grid cells that the item occupies in the player inventory.

columns.name: hasinv
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then the item will have its own inventory allowing for the capability to socket gems, runes, or jewels. If equals 0, then the item cannot have sockets.

columns.name: gemsockets
columns.description: Controls the maximum number of sockets allowed on this item. This is limited by the item's size based on the .invwidth and .invheight fields. This also compares with itemtypes.MaxSockets#.

columns.name: gemapplytype
columns.description: Determines which affect from a gem or rune will be applied when it is socketed into this item (See gems).

columns.name: flippyfile
columns.description: Controls which DC6 file to use for displaying the item in the game world when it is dropped on the ground (uses the file name as the input).

columns.name: invfile
columns.description: Controls which DC6 file to use for displaying the item graphics in the inventory (uses the file name as the input).

columns.name: uniqueinvfile
columns.description: Controls which DC6 file to use for displaying the item graphics in the inventory when it is a Unique quality item (uses the file name as the input).

columns.name: setinvfile
columns.description: Controls which DC6 file to use for displaying the item graphics in the inventory when it is a Set quality item (uses the file name as the input).

columns.name: rArm
columns.description: Controls the character's graphics and animations for the body components when wearing the armor. Corresponds to a enums.EARMORTYPE.

columns.name: lArm
columns.description: Controls the character's graphics and animations for the body components when wearing the armor. Corresponds to a enums.EARMORTYPE.

columns.name: Torso
columns.description: Controls the character's graphics and animations for the body components when wearing the armor. Corresponds to a enums.EARMORTYPE.

columns.name: Legs
columns.description: Controls the character's graphics and animations for the body components when wearing the armor. Corresponds to a enums.EARMORTYPE.

columns.name: rSPad
columns.description: Controls the character's graphics and animations for the body components when wearing the armor. Corresponds to a enums.EARMORTYPE.

columns.name: lSPad
columns.description: Controls the character's graphics and animations for the body components when wearing the armor. Corresponds to a enums.EARMORTYPE.

columns.name: useable
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then the item can be used with the right-click mouse button command (this only works with specific belt items or quest items). If equals 0, then ignore this.

columns.name: stackable
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then the item will use a quantity field and handle stacking functionality. This can depend on if the item type is throwable, is a type of ammunition, or is some other kind of miscellaneous item. If equals 0, then the item cannot be stacked. Community note: Not all items can be stacked if this property is turned on. Notably, gems/runes will crash the game if they are inserted into an item while they have a quantity.

columns.name: minstack
columns.description: Controls the minimum stack count or quantity that is allowed on the item. This field depends on the .stackable field being enabled.

columns.name: maxstack
columns.description: Controls the maximum stack count or quantity that is allowed on the item. This field depends on the .stackable field being enabled.

columns.name: spawnstack
columns.description: Controls the stack count or quantity that the item can spawn with. This field depends on the .stackable field being enabled.

columns.name: Transmogrify
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then the item will use the transmogrify function. If equals 0, then ignore this. This field depends on the .useable field being enabled. Community note: The Transmogrify behavior is unused in the base game, and is a relic from the alpha version of Diablo II, which allowed for the conversion of organs (dropped items) into various utility items. When Transmogrify is enabled, the item will get a piece of text in its tooltip, stating 'Right Click to Convert to X' and it will transform into another item. **Transmogrify only works on legacy versions of the game.**

columns.name: TMogType
columns.description: Links to a .code field to determine which item is chosen to transmogrify this item to.

columns.name: TMogMin
columns.description: Controls the minimum quantity that the transmogrify item will have. This depends on what item was chosen in the .TMogType field, and that the transmogrify item has quantity.

columns.name: TMogMax
columns.description: Controls the maximum quantity that the transmogrify item will have. This depends on what item was chosen in the .TMogType field, and that the transmogrify item has quantity.

columns.name: type
columns.description: This is the item's type, which controls how the item functions.

columns.name: type2
columns.description: Secondary item type. This is optional but can add more functionalities and possibilities with the item.

columns.name: dropsound
columns.description: Sound used when the item is dropped on the ground.

columns.name: dropsfxframe
columns.description: Defines which frame in the .flippyfile animation to play the .dropsound sound when the item is dropped on the ground.

columns.name: usesound
columns.description: Sound used when the item is moved in the inventory or used.

columns.name: unique
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then the item can only spawn as a Unique quality type. If equals 0, then the item can spawn as other quality types.

columns.name: transparent
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then the item will be drawn transparent on the player model (similar to ethereal models). If equals 0, then the item will appear solid on the player model.

columns.name: transtbl
columns.description: Controls what type of transparency to use, based on the .transparent field being enabled. Corresponds to a enums.ETRANSTYPE.

columns.name: lightradius
columns.description: Controls the value of the light radius that this item can apply on the monster. This only affects monsters with this item equipped, not other types of units. This is ignored if the item's component on the monster is "lit", "med", or "hvy".

columns.name: belt
columns.description: Controls which belt type to use for belt items only. This field determines what index entry in the belts file to use.

columns.name: quest
columns.description: Controls what quest class is tied to the item which can enable certain item functionalities for a specific quest. Any value greater than 0 will also mean the item is flagged as a quest item, which can affect how it is displayed in tooltips, how it is traded with other players, its item rarity, and how it cannot be sold to an NPC. If equals 0, then the item will not be flagged as a quest item.

columns.name: questdiffcheck
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1 and the .quest field is enabled, then the game will check the current difficulty setting and will tie that difficulty setting to the quest item. This means that the player can have more than 1 of the same quest item as long each they are obtained per difficulty mode (Normal / Nightmare / Hell). If equals 0 and the .quest field is enabled, then the player can only have 1 count of the quest item in the inventory, regardless of difficulty.

columns.name: missiletype
columns.description: Points to an entry's index in missiles, which determines what type of missile is used when using the throwing weapons

columns.name: durwarning
columns.description: Controls the threshold value for durability to display the low durability warning UI. This is only used if the item has durability.

columns.name: qntwarning
columns.description: Controls the threshold value for quantity to display the low quantity warning UI. This is only used if the item has stacks.

columns.name: mindam
columns.description: The minimum physical damage provided by the item.

columns.name: maxdam
columns.description: The maximum physical damage provided by the item.

columns.name: StrBonus
columns.description: The percentage multiplier that gets multiplied the player's current Strength attribute value to modify the bonus damage percent from the equipped item. If this equals 1, then default the value to 100.

columns.name: DexBonus
columns.description: The percentage multiplier that gets multiplied the player's current Dexterity attribute value to modify the bonus damage percent from the equipped item. If this equals 1, then default the value to 100.

columns.name: gemoffset
columns.description: Determines the starting index offset for reading the gems file when determining what effects gems or runes will have the item based on the .gemapplytype field. For example, if this value equals 9, then the game will start with index 9 ("Chipped Emerald") and ignore the previously defined gems in the gems.txt file, which can mean that those ignored gems will not apply modifiers when socketed into the item.

columns.name: bitfield1
columns.description: Controls different flags that can affect the item. Uses an integer value to check against different bit fields by using the "&" operator. For example, if the value equals 5 (binary = 101) then that returns true for both the 4 (binary = 100) and 1 (binary = 1) bit field values. Community note: This can be used to control which items can be consumed to make an Iron Golem.

columns.name: CharsiMin
columns.description: Minimum amount of this item type in Normal rarity that the NPC can sell at once.

columns.name: CharsiMax
columns.description: Maximum amount of this item type in Normal rarity that the NPC can sell at once. This must be equal to or greater than the minimum amount.

columns.name: CharsiMagicMin
columns.description: Minimum amount of this item type in Magical rarity that the NPC can sell at once

columns.name: CharsiMagicMax
columns.description: Maximum amount of this item type in Magical rarity that the NPC can sell at once. This must be equal to or greater than the minimum amount.

columns.name: CharsiMagicLvl
columns.description: Maximum magic level allowed for this item type in Magical rarity.

columns.name: GheedMin
columns.description: Minimum amount of this item type in Normal rarity that the NPC can sell at once.

columns.name: GheedMax
columns.description: Maximum amount of this item type in Normal rarity that the NPC can sell at once. This must be equal to or greater than the minimum amount.

columns.name: GheedMagicMax

columns.name: GheedMagicLvl
columns.description: Maximum magic level allowed for this item type in Magical rarity.

columns.name: AkaraMax

columns.name: AkaraMagicMax

columns.name: FaraMax

columns.name: HalbuMax

columns.name: MalahMax

columns.name: AnyaMin
columns.description: Minimum amount of this item type in Normal rarity that the NPC can sell at once.

columns.name: AnyaMax

columns.name: AnyaMagicMin
columns.description: Minimum amount of this item type in Magical rarity that the NPC can sell at once

columns.name: AnyaMagicMax

columns.name: AnyaMagicLvl
columns.description: Maximum magic level allowed for this item type in Magical rarity.

columns.name: Transform
columns.description: Controls the color palette change of the item for the character model graphics. Corresponds to a enums.EITRANSFORMCLASS.

columns.name: InvTrans
columns.description: Controls the color palette change of the item for the inventory graphics. Corresponds to a enums.EITRANSFORMCLASS.

columns.name: SkipName
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1 and the item is Unique rarity, then skip adding the item's base name in its title. If equals 0, then ignore this.

columns.name: NightmareUpgrade
columns.description: Links to another item's .code field. Used to determine which item will replace this item when being generated in the NPC's store while the game is playing in Nightmare difficulty. If this field's .code equals "xxx", then this item will not change in this difficulty.

columns.name: HellUpgrade
columns.description: Links to another item's .code field. Used to determine which item will replace this item when being generated in the NPC's store while the game is playing in Hell difficulty. If this field's .code equals "xxx", then this item will not change in this difficulty.

columns.name: Nameable
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then the item's name can be personalized by Anya for the Act 5 Betrayal of Harrogath quest reward. If equals 0, then the item cannot be used for the personalized name reward.

columns.name: PermStoreItem
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then this item will always appear on the NPC's store. If equals 0, then the item will randomly appear on the NPC's store when appropriate.

columns.name: UICatOverride
columns.description: Allows this item to override what UI Category it should use (for loot filter and chronicle uniques page). If there's no override, this item will belong to the category that its type1 field uses. If Null, this item will be excluded from lootfilter / chronicle. If a valid UI Category, this item will belong to that UI category instead of the one its type would usually put it in.

columns.name: diablocloneweight
columns.description: The amount of weight added to the diablo clone progress when this item is sold. When offline, selling this item will instead immediately spawn diablo clone.
